rag_rules: If AGREE reports type mismatches or unresolved identifiers, trace the issue to a visible AADL feature or declared helper on the same line and fix the root cause.
When repairing AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers and types are valid within the current component scope and match AADL data types.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use only visible identifiers and declared helpers; avoid introducing undefined or package-qualified references.
Repair AGREE diagnostics by aligning types and resolving root causes rather than adding new architecture elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must declare types explicitly as int, real, or bool.
Component type annexes are for assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new component ports or package-qualified identifiers in AGREE contracts that are not present in the AADL model.
Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE type errors; instead, correct the expression or helper declaration.
